document.docAuthor: Brescovit, Antonio Domingos, Zampaulo, Robson De Almeida, Cizauskas, Igor & Pedroso, Leandro
document.docTitle: Speocera babau Brescovit & Zampaulo & Cizauskas & Pedroso 2022, sp. nov.
document.masterDocTitle: Three new subterranean species of the genus Speocera (Araneae, Ochyroceratidae) from caves of the Carajás area in the state of Pará, Brazil

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Three new subterranean species of the genus Speocera (Araneae, Ochyroceratidae) from caves of the Carajás area in the state of Pará, Brazil

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Laboratório de Coleções Zoológicas, Instituto Butantan, Av. Vital Brasil, 1500, Butantã, São Paulo-São Paulo, Brazil, 05503 - 900 Gerencia de Licenciamento Ambiental e Espeleologia, Vale SA, Avenida Doutor Marco Paulo Simon Jardim, 34006 - 200 Nova Lima- Minas Gerais, Brazil
Organização de Apoio à Pesquisa da Biodiversidade (OAPBio), Rua Frei Inácio da Conceição 238, 05362 - 040, São Paulo, São Paulo, Brazil

Laboratório de Coleções Zoológicas, Instituto Butantan, Av. Vital Brasil, 1500, Butantã, São Paulo-São Paulo, Brazil, 05503 - 900 Gerencia de Licenciamento Ambiental e Espeleologia, Vale SA, Avenida Doutor Marco Paulo Simon Jardim, 34006 - 200 Nova Lima- Minas Gerais, Brazil

subSubSection.paragraph:
Type material .
Male holotype and female paratype from Cave N 1_0024 ( GEM –1220) ( 6°01’53”S 50°16’20”W ), Serra dos Carajás , Parauapebas , Pará , Brazil ,
11/VI – 02/VII/2014
, Equipe Carste et al. col., deposited in IBSP 207354;
Paratypes
: male and female from Cave N 1_0056 (GEM-1254) ( 6°01’11”S 50°16’44”W ), Serra dos Carajás , Parauapebas , Pará , Brazil ,
07 – 28/I/2015
, Equipe Carste et al. col. (ex IBSP 207537 ), deposited in MPEG 37251; two males and two females from Cave N 1_0226 ( 6°02’16”S 50°16’03”W ), Serra dos Carajás , Parauapebas , Pará , Brazil ,
04/IX – 06/X/2014
, Equipe Carste et al. col. (ex IBSP 207471 ), deposited in ISLA
.
Etymology . The specific name is a popular expression from Pará originated from the Tupi language for ended or exhausted. Noun in apposition.

Diagnosis .
Speocera babau
sp. nov.
differs by the male having SCI in the embolus narrow and elongated, dagger shape and projecting forward slender and with large base conductor in the palp (
Figs 7E – F
;
9A – C
)—while in
S. pinima
sp. nov.
it is pentagonal and the small conductor, fig. 1E, and very elongated and curved at tip, with shorter conductor in
S. piquira
sp. nov.
, fig. 12E. The female of
Speocera babau
sp. nov.
resemble those of
S. pinima
sp. nov.
by its long afferent spermathecae and elongated lateral plates (10D – E) but differs by basally projected spermathecae (
Fig. 10E – F
), curved distal afferent spermathecae (
Fig. 10E – F
) and enlarged and oval terminal depression ( Fig. 10D ) in the genitalia.

caption.paragraph:
FIGURE 7.
Speocera babau
sp. nov.
, male (IBSP 207488). A . Habitus, dorsal view. B–D . Left palp. B . Retrolateral view. C . Ventral view. D . Prolateral view. E–F . Embolus. E . Prolateral view. F. Ventral view. Abbreviations: B, bulb; C, conductor; SC, associated sclerites (SCI, SCII, SCIII and SCIV); SO, spermatic opening.

FIGURE 8.
Speocera babau
sp. nov.
, male (IBSP 207443), SEM images. A carapace, eyes, frontal view B Same, variation (IBSP 207491), frontal view C chelicerae, ventral view D Endites and labium, crosier-like macrosetae (arrow, detail), ventral view E epiandrous area, abdomen, ventral view F male palp, cymbium, tarsal organ, distal area.

FIGURE 9.
Speocera babau
sp. nov.
, male palp (IBSP 207443), SEM images. A bulb, retrolateral-ventral view B Embolus, retrolateral-ventral view C Same, ventral view D Same, prolateral-ventral view E Same, prolateral view F Same, spermatic opening, (arrow, detail), ventral view. Abbreviations: B, bulb; C, conductor; SC, associated sclerites (SCI, SCII, SCIII and SCIV); SO, spermatic opening.

FIGURE 10.
Speocera babau
sp. nov.
, female (IBSP 207456). A. Habitus, dorsal view. B–C. Abdomen. B. lateral view. C. Same, ventral view. D–F. Genitalia. D. Lateral view. E. enzyme cleared, dorsal view (IBSP 190032). F. Same, dorsal view. Abbreviations: AS, afferent spermathecae; CC, copulatory channel; LP, lateral plate; S, spermathecae; TD, terminal depression; VP, ventral plate.

subSubSection.paragraph: Variation . 10 ♂♂ : total length 0.9 – 1.1; carapace 0.4 – 0.6; femur I 0.8 – 0.9; 10 ♀♀ : total length 0.9 – 1.12; carapace 0.4 – 0.55; femur I 0.75 – 0.85.

Distribution . Widely distributed in iron caves the Carajás mountains, municipalities of Canaã dos Carajás, Curionópolis, Eldorado dos Carajás, Parauapebas and São Félix do Xingu, state of Pará , Brazil ( Fig. 19B, D ) in a range of approximately 170 km .

Natural history .
Speocera babau
sp. nov.
was commonly collected in the soil, generally found on floors, along roots and under blocks inside caves. Like other representatives of the genus and with other Ochyroceratidae , they have maternal behavior and carry the egg sac in the chelicera until hatching of the eggs ( Fig. 18C–D ).
